title: order-service latency spike root cause
lastMessage: Traced the latency to order-service calling inventory-service. inventory-service is responding in 11-13s because its Redis cache cluster failed over at 09:52 UTC and the fallback path queries PostgreSQL directly. Every order request makes 3-5 synchronous inventory checks with a 15s timeout — none are timing out yet, but they're all slow. Fix: circuit-break inventory-service calls and serve stale cache data during the Redis failover window.

title: inventory-service Redis failover investigation
lastMessage: Redis Cluster failover confirmed: primary node redis-master-0 failed at 09:52:05Z. Sentinel promoted redis-replica-1 but the promotion took 90 seconds during which all cache reads missed. inventory-service does not implement a circuit breaker for its Redis dependency — it falls through to synchronous Postgres queries. This causes the downstream cascade into order-service.

title: Redis cluster health
lastMessage: redis-master-0 OOM'd due to a missing maxmemory policy. The node was evicting keys for 10 minutes before crashing. Setting maxmemory-policy to allkeys-lru would have prevented the OOM crash.
